title: Relbunium bigeminum
taxonomy: Relbunium bigeminum is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Australia and is often found in the wild. Relbunium bigeminum is a relatively small plant with purple flowers and is often used in gardens for its unique appearance. The plant is a member of the Asclepiadoideae subfamily, which includes many species of milkweed and related plants. Further classification of Relbunium bigeminum may include it within the tribe Plumerieae.

intro: Relbunium bigeminum, also known as Twinflower, is a perennial flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its unique twin-petaled flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues.
